message:
fix(fcp-crypto): prevent polynomial degree reduction in Shamir secret sharing

The random polynomial generator for Shamir secret sharing could produce
a zero value for the highest-degree coefficient, silently reducing the
effective polynomial degree. This means a k-of-n threshold scheme could
become reconstructable with fewer than k shares, undermining the
security guarantee.

Now retries coefficient sampling until the leading term is non-zero,
ensuring the polynomial always has the intended degree and the
reconstruction threshold is enforced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
